core_mechanism: a stochastic birth-death-and-competition process among discrete competing entities sharing a finite population of adherents, where entities are created, grow or shrink by capturing members from rivals, and go extinct, self-organizing into a broad size distribution and a branching genealogy of variants
brief_reason: The abstract frames the method as a simulated competition between languages with birth, survival and death of entities, asking how many entities carry how many members and how they split into families, which is exactly a competitive birth-death/replacement dynamics on a shared population that generates a characteristic size distribution and branching lineage tree.
